sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Pitney Bowes Launches Temu Integration Within ShipAccel®
feed.summary: Pitney Bowes has launched a new Temu integration within its ShipAccel® solution, aiming to help U.S.-based sellers streamline fulfillment and shipping operations for their Temu marketplace businesses. This integration allows sellers to manage orders, generate shipping labels, and handle fulfillment workflows from a single platform, while also assisting with compliant onboarding to Temu. The initiative supports Pitney Bowes' goal of providing flexible tools for e-commerce sellers in an evolving marketplace.

feed.title: Rex American Resources 10-K: $650.5M Revenue, $2.50 EPS
feed.summary: Rex American Resources reported fiscal-year net sales of $650.5 million, a 1% increase year-over-year, and basic net income per share of $2.50. The company's profitability was supported by favorable crush spreads and 45Z tax credits, leading to a significant increase in net income attributable to shareholders. Rex American Resources is also planning substantial capital expenditure for expansion and carbon sequestration initiatives, with environmental permits still pending.

feed.title: Global X Copper Miners ETF (NY: COPX)
feed.summary: This article provides news headlines related to the Global X Copper Miners ETF (COPX). It includes recent stock performance data, showing COPX up +7.92% to 76.35, and a list of articles discussing metal markets, SPDR ETFs, copper prices, and the broader economy for early 2025. The content features analyses from Ira Epstein on gold and other metals, discussions on inflation, and the demand for critical metals.

feed.title: Gold Fields Ltd (GFI) Shares Up 4.54% on Mar 30
feed.summary: Gold Fields Ltd (GFI) saw its shares surge by 4.54% on March 30, closing at $42.34. While the stock is significantly above its 52-week low, it remains 31.31% below its 52-week high. Wall Street analysts maintain an "Outperform" rating with an average target price of $55.00, suggesting a potential upside of 29.90%.

feed.title: Former AMN chief who led 26 acquisitions joins Teladoc board
feed.summary: Teladoc Health (NYSE: TDOC) has appointed Susan Salka, former president and CEO of AMN Healthcare, to its board of directors, effective March 30, 2026. Salka, who led 26 acquisitions and grew AMN Healthcare's revenue to over $5 billion, will serve on Teladoc's audit and compensation committees. Her appointment follows the retirements of two board members and is expected to bring significant healthcare leadership and M&A experience to Teladoc.

feed.title: ARKO Petroleum Corp. Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Results
feed.summary: ARKO Petroleum Corp. (Nasdaq: APC) announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2025, reporting increased net income and adjusted EBITDA for the quarter, though full-year net income decreased. The company highlighted the successful completion of its IPO in February 2026, which raised $206.7 million to reduce debt, and detailed the ongoing conversion of ARKO Retail Sites to dealer locations and plans for new fleet fueling sites. ARKO Petroleum also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.26 per share.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) CEO family trust sells 113,221 shares at $132.71
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips CEO Ryan Lance's family trust sold 113,221 shares of common stock at a weighted average price of $132.7085 per share on March 31, 2026. This open market transaction, reported via a Form 4 SEC filing, involved shares worth approximately $15 million. After the sale, the trust still indirectly holds 350,000 shares, while Lance also retains direct holdings and units in the company's savings plan.

feed.title: 200,000 Tennessee gas customers move to Spire in $2.48B deal
feed.summary: Duke Energy has completed the sale of its Tennessee Piedmont Natural Gas business to Spire for $2.48 billion. This transaction includes nearly 3,800 miles of pipelines serving over 200,000 customers, with Piedmont employees transitioning to Spire. The proceeds will fund a substantial portion of Duke Energy's $103 billion regulated capital plan and pay down Piedmont debt.

feed.title: Spire completes acquisition of Tennessee Piedmont Natural Gas business from Duke Energy
feed.summary: Spire Inc. has completed its acquisition of the Piedmont Natural Gas business in Tennessee from Duke Energy for $2.48 billion, expanding its regulated utility footprint. The newly formed Spire Tennessee will be the largest investor-owned natural gas utility in the state, serving over 200,000 customers. This acquisition is expected to support Spire's long-term adjusted earnings per share growth and involves more than 200 Piedmont employees transitioning to Spire.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Keeps Buy Opinion On Shares Of Constellation Energy Corporation
feed.summary: CFRA has maintained a 'Buy' opinion on shares of Constellation Energy Corporation (CEG). The article also briefly mentions Unity Software's (U.US) fourth-quarter financial results, reporting revenue of $609 million which beat estimates, and a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: 3,639 RSUs registered; CYTK (NASDAQ: CYTK) insider 10b5-1 sales listed
feed.summary: Cytokinetics (NASDAQ: CYTK) has filed a Form 144 to register the proposed sale of 3,639 Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) dated March 6, 2026. The filing also details multiple pre-arranged 10b5-1 sales by insider Andrew Callos between January 2, 2026, and March 18, 2026, including transactions of 15,000 shares and 11,000 shares on March 5, 2026, with reported proceeds. This indicates routine insider trading activity rather than information-based trading.

feed.title: Cytokinetics (CYTK) EVP sells 3,639 shares, retains 66,004
feed.summary: Andrew Callos, EVP and Chief Commercial Officer of Cytokinetics (CYTK), sold 3,639 shares of Common Stock at $65.00 per share on March 31, 2026. Following this transaction, Callos directly holds 66,004 shares of Cytokinetics Common Stock. This open-market sale, detailed in a Form 4 filing, amounted to $236,535.

feed.title: Loews (L) director Jonathan C. Locker gets 235-share quarterly equity grant
feed.summary: Jonathan C. Locker, a director at Loews Corporation (L), received a quarterly equity grant of 235 shares of common stock for his board service. These shares were awarded at no cash cost under the company's 2025 Incentive Compensation Plan, increasing his direct holdings to 25,225 common shares. This transaction was reported in a Form 4 filing and is part of his director compensation.

feed.title: A Look At Teledyne Technologies (TDY) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Teledyne Technologies (TDY) has experienced recent stock price weakness, declining 14.2% over the past month, while still showing positive longer-term returns. Despite being considered 16.5% undervalued by Simply Wall St's narrative valuation model, its P/E ratio is higher than the industry average, creating valuation tension. The article suggests further investigation into Teledyne's risk and reward balance and recommends using Simply Wall St's screener for other investment opportunities.

feed.title: Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) director receives 100-share restricted stock grant
feed.summary: Elizabeth H. Mitchell, a director at Principal Financial Group (NYSE: PFG), received a grant of 100 shares of common stock as equity compensation, described as restricted stock units. This transaction, reported on a Form 4, increased her direct holdings to 10,945 shares. The grant was compensation-related, with no cash cost per share, aligning the director's interests with long-term shareholder value.

feed.title: Director at Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) receives grant of 264 restricted shares
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group director Diane C. Nordin was granted 264 restricted stock units (RSUs) of common stock on March 27, 2026. This equity award, with a grant price of $0.00 per share, increased her direct holdings to 28,945 shares. The transaction was reported in a Form 4 filing, indicating it was an award rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) director receives stock and phantom unit awards
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group director Scott Mills received 182 phantom stock units and 307 restricted common stock units, which convert one-for-one into shares. These are routine equity awards, not open-market trades. Following these grants, his direct holdings increased to 33,712 common shares and 19,926 phantom stock units.

feed.title: Director at Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) receives 203-share award
feed.summary: Jonathan Auerbach, a director at Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG), received an equity award of 203 shares of Common Stock as restricted stock units on March 27, 2026. This transaction, classified as a grant with a $0.00 per share price, increased his directly held shares to 20,611. The filing indicates a routine, compensation-related addition rather than an open-market purchase or sale, aligning insider interests with shareholders through equity compensation.

feed.title: Fortive (FTV) CFO boosts deferred stake with new EDIP phantom shares
feed.summary: Fortive Corp's CFO, Mark D. Okerstrom, acquired an additional 3.732 phantom stock units through the company’s Executive Deferred Incentive Program (EDIP). These units represent notional dividend accruals tied to Fortive common stock, increasing his balance in the Fortive Stock Fund to 3,358.15 phantom shares. The phantom shares mirror the value of common stock and will settle in shares upon meeting specific vesting and employment conditions.

feed.title: Fortive (FTV) chief legal officer gains EDIP phantom stock accruals
feed.summary: Fortive Corp's SVP and chief legal officer, Peter C. Underwood, acquired 15.48 phantom shares in the Executive Deferred Incentive Program's Fortive Stock Fund through notional dividend accruals. These shares, valued at $53.92 each, increase his total phantom share balance to 13,923.10. Phantom shares track Fortive common stock on a one-to-one basis and are part of a non-qualified deferred compensation plan.

feed.title: Newmont will post Q1 results after market close on April 23
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NYSE: NEM) will release its first quarter 2026 results after North American markets close on Thursday, April 23, 2026, with a conference call scheduled for 5:30 p.m. Eastern Daylight Time the same day. The webcast details and materials will be available on the company's investor relations website. Newmont, the world's leading gold company, is also a producer of copper, zinc, lead, and silver, with a strong presence in various mining jurisdictions globally.

feed.title: Labcorp will post first-quarter results before markets open April 30
feed.summary: Labcorp (NYSE: LH) is scheduled to report its first-quarter 2026 financial results before the market opens on April 30, 2026. The company will also host a live webcast at 9:00 a.m. ET on the same day to discuss the results. The earnings release and financial information will be made available on Labcorp's investor relations website, with a replay of the webcast accessible for one year.

feed.title: DoubleDown Interactive (NASDAQ: DDI) outlines casino reliance, platform risk and IFRS shift
feed.summary: DoubleDown Interactive has filed its annual report, revealing significant reliance on its flagship DoubleDown Casino game, which generated 76.1% of its 2025 revenue. The company also highlighted its heavy dependence on third-party platforms like Apple and Google, and detailed its transition to IFRS accounting standards beginning in 2024. Furthermore, DoubleDown Interactive detailed legal and regulatory risks, including U.S. lawsuits regarding social casino games and data privacy concerns, while also mentioning its recent acquisitions of European iGaming operator SuprNation and German social casino developer WHOW Games.

feed.title: Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) CEO gets new stock unit grants
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group's Chair, President, and CEO, Deanna D. Strable-Soethout, received equity awards on March 27, 2026. She was granted 680 shares of common stock, increasing her direct holdings to 173,255 shares, and an additional 52 shares were awarded to an account held by her spouse, bringing that indirect position to 53,498 shares. These were restricted stock units, not open-market purchases, and were compensation-related grants at a price of $0.00 per share.

feed.title: State Street (STT) hires C. Jack Read as new chief accounting officer
feed.summary: State Street Corporation has appointed C. Jack Read as its new Executive Vice President, Global Controller, and Chief Accounting Officer, effective August 10, 2026. He will succeed Elizabeth M. Schaefer and brings extensive senior finance and risk experience from previous roles at MSCI Inc., Citizens Financial Group, and JPMorgan Chase, among others. Read's compensation package includes an annualized base salary of $450,000, a target 2026 total incentive award of $2,100,000, and one-time transition payments totaling $2,430,000.

feed.title: [Form 4] Torrid Holdings Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Torrid Holdings Inc.'s Chief Operating Officer, Hyon C. Park, reported a tax-related share disposition. On March 27, 2026, 3,848 shares of Common Stock were withheld at $1.79 per share to cover tax liabilities from restricted stock vesting, not a market sale. After this, Park directly holds 350,914 shares, indicating the transaction was compensation- and tax-driven rather than a discretionary trading decision.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Recommends Shareholders Reject “Mini-Tender” Offer by Potemkin Limited
feed.summary: GE HealthCare (Nasdaq: GEHC) has advised its shareholders to reject an unsolicited mini-tender offer from Potemkin Limited to purchase up to 100,000 shares at $45.80 each. This price is approximately 34.80% below the market closing price of $70.25 on March 18, 2026. The company highlights that mini-tender offers often bypass SEC disclosure requirements and may not provide adequate investor protections.

feed.title: Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) executive receives 660-share equity grant in Form 4 filing
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group executive Christopher J. Littlefield received a 660-share restricted stock unit award at no cost, increasing his direct holdings to 75,880 shares. This equity grant, detailed in a Form 4 filing, is a compensation award rather than a market transaction. Littlefield's holdings also include 2,984 shares from the company’s Employee Stock Purchase Plan and an additional 11 shares held indirectly in a revocable trust.

feed.title: Xylem to Release First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on April 28, 2026
feed.summary: Xylem Inc. (NYSE: XYL) will release its first quarter 2026 financial results on April 28, 2026, at 6:55 a.m. ET. Following the release, Xylem's senior management will host a conference call with investors at 9:00 a.m. ET to discuss the results. A replay of the briefing will be available online and via telephone until May 12, 2026.

feed.title: After 2 weeks, Palisade’s Crohn’s pill showed no serious side effects
feed.summary: Palisade Bio reported positive topline Phase 1b data for its Crohn's disease drug, PALI-2108, which demonstrated favorable safety and tolerability with no serious adverse events over a 14-day study period. The drug showed significant endoscopic improvements, including a 47.5% reduction in SES-CD scores and a 40% endoscopic response and remission rate. The company plans to advance PALI-2108 into a Phase 2 trial for moderate-to-severe Crohn’s disease, aiming to assess anti-fibrotic effects and further validate its potential as a targeted therapy.

feed.title: Torrid Holdings (CURV) CFO withholds shares to cover tax on vesting
feed.summary: Torrid Holdings Inc.'s CFO, Paula Dempsey, reported a tax-related disposition where 2,289 shares of common stock were withheld at $1.79 per share to satisfy tax liabilities from restricted stock vesting. This transaction, detailed in a Form 4 SEC filing, is a standard procedure for equity compensation and not an open-market sale. Following the withholding, Dempsey directly holds 273,071 shares of common stock.

feed.title: Elastic, PagerDuty, nCino, Atlassian, and Commerce Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Shares of Elastic, PagerDuty, nCino, Atlassian, and Commerce surged in the afternoon after President Trump indicated productive talks with Iran, signaling a de-escalation of Middle Eastern tensions. This improved market sentiment, coupled with investors "buying the dip" in high-quality SaaS stocks after a recent correction, drove the gains. The article details the percentage jump for each company and provides further context on Elastic's recent volatility and year-to-date performance.

feed.title: Principal Financial (NYSE: PFG) Asia president granted 667 stock units
feed.summary: Principal Financial Group executive Cheong Wee Yee, President of Asia & Middle East, was granted 667 shares of common stock at a zero transaction price, indicating a compensation-related award. Following this transaction, Cheong beneficially owns a total of 92,188 shares, which includes 1,763 shares acquired through the company's Employee Stock Purchase Plan. This insider transaction was reported via a Form 4 filing, providing transparency on executive stock holdings.

feed.title: [Form 4] Castellum, Inc. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: Castellum, Inc.'s Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer, David T. Bell, acquired 1,329 shares of common stock through the company's 2025 Employee Stock Purchase Plan. This transaction increased his direct holdings to 16,053 shares. The shares were purchased at $0.4544 per share, reflecting a 15% discount to the closing price on March 30, 2026, indicating a routine, compensation-related acquisition rather than an open-market trade.

feed.title: Castellum (NYSE: CTM) COO boosts stake through purchase plan
feed.summary: Castellum, Inc.'s Chief Operating Officer, Andrew Merriman, purchased 1,329 shares of common stock through the company's 2025 Employee Stock Purchase Plan at $0.4544 per share. This transaction increased his direct holdings to 537,329 shares. The purchase was made at a 15% discount to the closing price on the NYSE American on March 30, 2026, as reported in a recent SEC Form 4 filing.

feed.title: Udemy-Coursera merger supplements proxy; COUR stockholder vote April 9, 2026
feed.summary: Udemy and Coursera have issued supplemental disclosures for their planned merger, addressing three lawsuits and demand letters challenging the transaction. Both companies maintain that the claims lack merit and have provided additional details "without admitting any culpability," aiming to resolve perceived disclosure deficiencies and avoid delays. The stockholder vote for the merger is scheduled for April 9, 2026, where shareholders will decide on Udemy becoming a wholly owned subsidiary of Coursera.

feed.title: Masco (NYSE: MAS) proxy details CEO pay plan, board addition Coombe
feed.summary: Masco Corporation's recent proxy statement outlines its plans for its 2026 Annual Meeting, highlighting changes to CEO executive compensation, including a shift to forward-looking, time-based restricted stock units for 2025, and details the appointment of Gary A. Coombe to its board. The company's 2025 operational performance, compensation outcomes, and various corporate governance matters such as board refreshment, risk oversight, and proposed amendments to its Certificate of Incorporation are also covered. Shareholders will vote on director nominees, executive compensation, auditor ratification, and several management proposals, including a shareholder proposal regarding the ability to call special meetings.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate (NYSE: ARE) adopts simple majority for director removal
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, Inc. (NYSE: ARE) has amended its corporate governance to allow for director removal with a simple majority vote of shareholders, moving away from a previous Maryland state law requiring a two-thirds vote. This change, effective March 31, 2026, was enacted through Articles Supplementary after shareholders approved a "Simple Majority Vote" proposal at the 2025 annual meeting. The company filed an 8-K to detail this material event, signifying a shift in shareholder power regarding board composition.

feed.title: Edison International will discuss first-quarter results on April 28
feed.summary: Edison International (NYSE: EIX) has scheduled its first-quarter financial results conference call for Tuesday, April 28, 2026, from 1:30 to 2:30 p.m. PT. Investors and interested parties can access the call via specific phone numbers for U.S. and international callers, or through a webcast on the company's investor relations website. A telephone replay will also be available until May 12.

feed.title: [8-K] VERTEX PHARMACEUTICALS INC / MA Reports Material Event
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals has completed the submission of its rolling Biologics License Application (BLA) to the FDA for povetacicept, seeking accelerated approval for the treatment of immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) in adults. The company utilized a priority review voucher, which is expected to reduce the FDA's review period to six months instead of the standard ten months. This move transitions the drug from late-stage development to formal regulatory review, potentially leading to an earlier approval decision.

feed.title: Apollo Circles KKR's Atlantic Aviation In $10 Billion Acquisition Move
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management is reportedly in advanced talks to acquire Atlantic Aviation from KKR & Co., a deal that could value the private jet fixed-base operator at around $10 billion. Apollo is partnering with GIC Pte for a controlling stake, while KKR may still inject new capital to retain its interest. This potential acquisition follows Apollo's recent announcement of selling CooIT Systems and KKR's previous acquisition of CooIT in 2023.

feed.title: Regions Financial, Live Oak Bancshares, KeyCorp, First Horizon, and Fifth Third Bancorp Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Several regional bank stocks, including Regions Financial, Live Oak Bancshares, KeyCorp, First Horizon, and Fifth Third Bancorp, saw an afternoon rally following reports of a potential de-escalation of military hostilities with Iran. This news eased investor concerns about geopolitical risk, suggesting a more stable environment for the financial sector, which could lead to improved loan growth, credit quality, and consumer spending. The article also specifically examines KeyCorp's recent trajectory and its previous reaction to news regarding the private credit market.

feed.title: T-Mobile (Illinois) WARN Act Investigation
feed.summary: Strauss Borrelli PLLC is investigating T-Mobile USA Inc. for a potential violation of the federal WARN Act due to a recent mass layoff in Schaumburg, Illinois. The firm claims T-Mobile may have failed to provide the required 60 days' notice before laying off 172 employees, potentially entitling those employees to 60 days of severance pay and benefits. The WARN Act mandates that employers with 100 or more employees provide a 60-day notice for significant layoffs or plant closings.

feed.title: S&P Global, Cambridge Associates and Mercer Launch Private Markets Performance Datasets for Private Credit and Real Assets
feed.summary: S&P Global, in collaboration with Cambridge Associates and Mercer, has launched new Private Markets Performance Analytics datasets for private credit and real assets. This initiative provides standardized, anonymized data for thousands of funds through the iLEVEL platform, aiming to improve performance comparison, risk management, and allocation decisions for investors. Future releases will expand coverage to private equity and other asset classes later in 2026.

feed.title: Colliers investors re-elect board, approve 1.5M-share option increase
feed.summary: Colliers (TSX: CIGI; NASDAQ: CIGI) announced that shareholders re-elected all ten director nominees and approved PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as auditor during a virtual annual meeting on March 31, 2026. Shareholders also approved a non-binding advisory resolution on executive compensation and an amendment to increase the stock option plan reserve by 1,500,000 subordinate voting shares. This news reflects ongoing governance continuity and balance-sheet actions for the global professional services and investment management company.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (NYSE: COP) EVP Nicholas Olds makes bona fide gift of 1,903 shares
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips Executive Vice President Nicholas G. Olds reported a bona fide gift of 1,903 shares of COP common stock, transferred at $0.00 per share as a charitable disposition. Following this transaction, Olds directly holds 3,492 shares and indirectly holds an additional 1,361.969 shares through the ConocoPhillips Savings Plan. This gift is not considered an open-market sale or purchase.

feed.title: Halliburton (NYSE: HAL) director adds stock equivalent and RSU awards
feed.summary: Halliburton director Maurice S. Smith recently received 827.815 stock equivalent units under the company's Directors' Deferred Compensation Plan. These units, which convert one-for-one into common stock, are settled after his board service concludes and reflect quarterly dividends and fees. Following this grant, Smith directly holds 13,768.454 stock equivalent units and various restricted stock unit awards.

feed.title: Catalyst Bancorp 2025 10-K: $13.90M Interest Income, $0.56 Diluted EPS
feed.summary: Catalyst Bancorp reported a significant improvement in its 2025 financial performance, achieving net income of $2.052 million ($0.56 diluted EPS) compared to a prior-year loss. This turnaround was driven by stable interest income, increased net interest margin, lower credit loss provisions, and reduced non-interest expenses, especially an absence of securities sale losses. The company also shifted its strategy towards relationship-based commercial banking and enhanced its digital services.

feed.title: Saudi Aramco-Dow JV suspends chemical output amid regional disruptions (DOW:NYSE)
feed.summary: A joint venture between Saudi Aramco and Dow, Sadara Chemical Co., has temporarily halted production at a major petrochemical facility in Saudi Arabia. This suspension highlights how growing Middle East tensions are impacting industrial operations and supply chains. The move affects both Saudi Aramco (ARMCO) and Dow (DOW).

feed.title: Comstock Resources (NYSE:CRK) Stock Price Down 5.2% - Here's Why
feed.summary: Comstock Resources (NYSE: CRK) saw its stock price decline by 5.2% on Tuesday, trading at $20.74, despite beating Q4 earnings and revenue expectations. The company reported EPS of $0.16 against an estimated $0.11 and revenue of $787.32 million, up 115.5% year-over-year. Analyst sentiment remains mixed, with a consensus "Reduce" rating and an average price target of $21.00.
